# Test showing that standard callgraph does NOT stitch test scripts to programs

# Create a simple test that calls mcpdiff
exec mcpdiff --help
stderr 'Usage'

# Create a simple Go test that uses exec.Command
mkdir -p test_project

# Create the test file using echo and redirects instead of setstdin
exec echo 'package main_test' > test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo 'import (' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '    "os/exec"' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '    "testing"' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo ')' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo 'func TestCallsMCPDiff(t *testing.T) {' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '    cmd := exec.Command("mcpdiff", "--version")' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '        t.Fatal(err)' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '    }' >> test_project/test_example.go
exec echo '}' >> test_project/test_example.go

# If we ran standard callgraph on this, it would show:
# - The test calls exec.Command
# - But NOT what specific binary (mcpdiff) is executed
# - And NOT the connection to mcpdiff's main() function

# This is the gap our testcallgraph tool will fix
